mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 1% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicates deep accommodation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 7% signals labor market slack and reduced wage pressure, which lowers corporate cost structures and increases profit margins, producing positive equity returns over 60 trading days as market reprices earnings expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 7.3% indicates elevated slack
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market weakness
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when UNRATE > 7% are not statistically significantly higher than when UNRATE ≤ 7% (t-test p > 0.10), or correlation reverses sign over any 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline month-over-month while housing starts fall below 900k units signals demand contraction that threatens corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 426563M and HOUST at 896k suggest demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (both conditions met) shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) over any 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index falls below 99 while payroll employment declines month-over-month indicates simultaneous supply-side contraction and labor shedding, signaling recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.95 near threshold, PAYEMS at 136133k
failure_condition: Joint signal (both conditions met) fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy over any 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02 when both conditions are simultaneously triggered.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI rises above 235 index points while federal funds rate remains below 0.5% indicates real rates deeply negative, compressing bond yields and driving equity risk premium compression that produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 233.32 near threshold, FEDFUNDS at 0.08%
regime_hint: financial repression regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns when both conditions are met do not significantly exceed baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10), or the interaction effect disappears when tested on out-of-sample data.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 50k new jobs per month while unemployment rate rises above 7.2% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as consumption forecasts are revised downward.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration with demand weakness
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 when both conditions are simultaneously active over any 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 850k units signals residential investment contraction that reduces aggregate demand and forward GDP growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised lower.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 896k currently above threshold but approaching
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns when HOUST < 850 are not significantly lower than when HOUST ≥ 850 (t-test p > 0.10), or the relationship reverses sign over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines below 98.5 signals supply-side weakness and reduced productive capacity utilization, indicating stagflation risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as profit margin compression concerns emerge.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO < 98.5, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
